reviews.review_title: Way Too Expensive For What You Recieve
reviews.review_description:
If you're going to consider buying this buy the Steam version when its on sale. Legendary Tales has very good combat and customizable skill trees but it's not worth freaking 42 dollars for poor graphics, sparse magical abilities, mediocre bow shooting , no randomly generated dungeons (For an Unreal Engine 4/5 game with low fidelity game models this is easily done and coincidentally will be released as a paid DLC in the future on steam), movement mechanics are very slow (strafing side to side or backstepping is slow for a melee game, very reliant on a 3  second cooldown dash mechanic), NO CROSSPLAY WITH PCVR AND PSVR, and lastly the multiplayer is P2P so 80% of the time you're out of sync with the host and your experience will be very poor. We already pay enough for the headset itself and even the DLC thats already on steam is an added 20 dollars. It is also known that due to poor sales on Meta Store the DLCs for Legendary Tales will NOT be coming to the Meta Quest. Due to this game's price the active player base for Meta Quest can be counted with a single hand. 

To the Dev: BJ, You and your small South Korean studio are not selling a gold plated collectors item of a VR game (In the past this game was 55 dollars [wtf] on steam at full release for years before a price readjustment) when the visuals and animations look like they've been pooted out of the early 2000's and content is sparse in comparison to other games with the same price range. Your combat work and your skill tree are very good! Lower the price so you can get more public exposure to fund later content releases!!

For the customer reading this: Buy Dungeons of Eternity for 30 bucks or when it goes on sale for the meantime; more content, more players, and almost as good combat with better ranged combat "ergonomics" (not as good skill trees) with cross play capabilities with players on other platforms.

reviews.review_title: One my my personal favorite games
reviews.review_description:
Open world ✅
Cool magic casting and skill tree allowing for different class builds✅
Physic based combat ✅
Multiplayer✅
Crafting✅
Trading system✅
Bosses✅

I don’t understand the hate this game gets it’s genuinely a great dark soul clone and as a dark souls/berserk fan this game scratches the itch

Most of the negative reviews I have seen on this game regarded managing your inventory which if you find to be a hassle you simply didn't play the tutorial as there is a feature specifically implemented to make it easy to retrieve items during combat but for my opinions I believe the game is great it has amazing physics paired with lots of play styles customization along with a lot of cool and unique abilities and while I can agree that the bosses have pretty basic attack sets during your initial play through they are all unique and fun and they become more challenging and unique as there is new game +. This is a great game very fun and definitely worth it if medieval combat is your thing but I recommend disregarding the negative reviews and simply play the tutorial and mess with your controls until you figure them out
Please ignore this game entirely. Buy it on some other platform. It will not be getting the DLC here. SKIP RIGHT PAST THIS ONE. There are other games that deserve your money.

This game is so far pretty good but as soon as I started playing I noticed something that immediately took the immersion away. I always when I start a game, clank my blades together to get into the mood of slaying and with this game, no metal clanking sound. Wtf? This also goes with blades touching stone, walls or other objects. It's crazy how something so simple will pull you in or out of an immersive experience. I mean as soon as I saw this, I almost turned it off and wanted my money back. But I'm still playing hopeful that this will be fixed. The game is challenging so far, and it has great potential. Please fix

I have to say this was quite challenging game at first but turns out I’ve made a friend who helped me a ton and is an ACE at this game. What’s great about him is he helped me boost my characters skill and helped me defeat the final boss I was stuck on. So if you’re ever in a pickle I urge you to add Tarquinn2049 as a friend and he’ll help you design your character to your liking like he did mine. But as for this game it has too many issues for instance the ragdoll physics of this game is bad when attacking the enemy it doesn’t register it hitting the enemy a quarter of the time the enemies have auto aim and much much more. So if you’re interested I recommend you do the free version first before you buy and see what you think of it. Solo campaign will take you a long time but multiplayer you’ll buzz through it quicker.

reviews.review_title: Good start but the end is disappointing
reviews.review_description:
UPD: The save file appeared the next day, so the bug is not so critical in the end. Also, I have advanced in the game and found a few things of concern: an obscure progression system and the balance. At some point the game becomes much much harder - mobs hit you through blocks, the damage increases too, and the amount of enemies rises exponentially. And I still don't understand how to upgrade myself to tackle it. I put almost all of my points into STR but it didn't help. I crafted new swords matching my character's level and it helped but not so much. I got some perks from the upgrade tree but again, almost no result. I don't understand the upgrade system and which parts cause which effects. It's so convoluted without any reason. The quantity of perks is overwhelming - it would be better to have fewer but more useful ones. I can still advance only because of the skills I acquired playing Blade & Sorcery for years. And, OMG, the final level and the final boss are just an "old good" grindwall. That's the reason why I'm dropping the game and not going to finish it. I enjoyed it most of the time till the last levels but grindwalls in VR games are a shame.
Is this game good? Yes, like 70% of the game, it's much better than most VR games. The physics system is good. The combat system in general is good. The atmosphere is good but the further you go, the more tiring it becomes. The game has a story and, let's admit, that's an exception for VR games and definitely an advantage. Overall the game is good but needs some polishing and an overhaul of the final levels to make it truly stand out among other top VR games like Batman. 4 of 5.
old review:
In the Blighthaven where you meet the goblin from the tutorial, I tried to do some alchemy but the game freezes and only a hard reset helps. But after the headset reload all saves are lost and I have to start from the beginning. That's sad.

It wants to be Dark Souls but it is too easy. The fights tend to be the same and the bosses just suck. I bought this game because maybe it was better than Blade and Sorcery (it wasn’t.)The only good factor is that you can play with friends, in which case it’s the only reason why you would want to play this title. Managing your inventory feels terrible, gameplay is very simple, and whatever story line this game has is not great. The trailer shows this game to you like it is intriguing, but it is far from the truth. REALLY THINK ABOUT IT BEFORE YOU BUY!!!

reviews.review_title: Legendary tales best game ever but...
reviews.review_description: I love this game very much it is the best game I ever played. Only two problems I have with this game is at least it has crashed once when I was fighting some skeletons a couple of months ago and when I got back to camp after completing the first dungeon blight town and talked to the female merchant npc and selected the option rewards it did nothing at all I am stuck trying to get through the dialouge by reseting the game and the headset but nothing worked I still could not advance the story I need this patched up so I can continue my adventure. But make no mistake this is best game I ever played I am just stuck that is all and always remember you guys have done a great job with making this game never give up and otherwise 5/5 stars.

Im sorry but I just do not get the hype on this pricey game, seems to be just a wave sword fighting game not much to do some loot and upgrades but nothing jumps out not much of a story other than reading stuff area's not very entertaining imho.  Im not a fan of sword fighting seemingly endless skelly armies over and over in numbers, gets old fast , I got bored in a hr, might be decent with a friend I guess but for 40 bucks none of my frs are interested in a sword fighting game, bows are decent but lack power as they do little to the bigger skellys . Also very difficult to operate inventory system,  storing stuff is almost impossible mechanically and makes no sense on how to store and retrieve,  couldn't get any store most the time nor retrieve much at all.

Huge fan of dungeons of eternity. Came over to this because a friend wanted to try it. This game is comically bad and almost unplayable. Made it through the tutorial, after having to restart the game due to glitches 3 times. Then started the game and was not surprised at how bad it was. It’s got a long way to go before it’s worth the money I wasted on it.

If you like games akin to diablo then you’re going to love this one, the developer is nothing but passionate about the game and his community and it shows. Legendary tales is one of the better VR games on the market right now, the sword fighting is physics based but isn’t as janky as B&S, the co-op community is extremely nice, the bosses are difficult and well made, the legendary weapons are all fun to use in their own way, and the progression feels steady as a mix of your skill and your gear. This game is truly a hidden gem and  i will be recommending it to my friends.

reviews.review_title: Not impressed at all. All lie reviews
reviews.review_description: This game literally wants $60 for PS1 polygon shaped faces, trash graphics all around, physics aren’t impressive at all. Then on top of that it’s a bloody nightmare just to try and holster weapons behind your back. At best this game is maybe worth $20 in its current state. I find all reviewers were probably the ones that made this. After paying less for games Like Grimlord, where I can literally climb walks with my knives. Bonelab, Blade and Sorcery. All cheaper, and far better quality games, this game doesn’t even compare to any of them at all. Sorry devs for the harsh review, but I’m a blunt speaker, and this game is overpriced garbage at this point. Feel like anyone that gave this game a 5 stars,must be the ones who made it, or goofs that have never experienced anything else.
I logged in one day to find my character gone fix your game
Will the expansions be coming to Meta?
needs the dlc

reviews.review_title: What VR dungeon crawling SHOULD be!
reviews.review_description:
Although Legendary Tales JUST dropped on Quest today, I've logged over 300 hours playing on PC. The devs truly made a hidden gem. Physics combat in co-op, with TOUGH enemies and difficult bosses. An amazing soundtrack and spell gestures that just make sense.

The dev team clearly cares about their community, as shown by many of our suggestions making it into the game after consideration from the lead dev.

If I had any complaint, it would be that the game is a little dark visually, but considering that it's basically VR Diablo/Dark Souls, it fits.


Get this game. Come adventure with me!
I can't stop playing the game

This is the open world game that quest has needed for a long time! It’s a full open world RPG with a huge variety of weapons and quests. The weapons are physics based and feel amazing and natural to use like you’re actually fighting. Im a huge fan of Dungeons of Eternity. The way I would describe this is having dungeons of eternity style combat with more realism and physics combined with an open world to explore with quests. The combat requires skill rather than wiggle sword fighting, and hitting and parrying feel weighty and physics based. This is easily the best, most satisfying combat I’ve ever done in vr. The fact that it’s multiplayer makes it so much better. I’ve only played about an hour of the game so far, but my first impressions so far are great. Will update this review as I progress!

UPDATE: Just defeated the Skeleton Boss and The Necromancer! The more you play, the better it gets! The combat with sword and shields is extremely realistic and satisfying and every strike and parry feels natural and impactful. You get better the more you play at it and it never gets old.  Exploring the open world is always exciting as new discoveries await, and the quests keep you going and motivated. The boss fights are one of the most challenging and epic things I’ve ever done in a vr game and the sense of accomplishment that comes from defeating one is unreal. All in all, this is the best quest game I’ve ever played in vr with the most realistic combat. Highly recommend for anyone who likes exploration and fighting games!

This is absolutely the most immersive VR game I have ever played in my life! If you want to experience actual sword fighting, and taking down challenges foes with the weapons that you hammered yourself at an anvil then this is absolutely the game for you! I genuinely cried during the ending because I was so sad that I had finished the game, and that the best week of my life was over. But it turns out that this game actually has a ton to do even after you beat it! So theres an insane amount of content to play in this game, and if you want to experience an actual week long journey of exploring dungeons and fightibg challengibg bosses, then this is definitely the game for you!

Overall a good game. I prefer Blade and sorcery, but if you enjoy that chances are you like this too.

Pay close attention to the tutorial, because some controls are not intuitive.

this is truly one the greatest vr games of all time. i’ve played countless hours on the pc and even there i barely scratched the surface of everything you can do. the combat feels so satisfying and the weapon variation and magic system is very nice. on top of that the game looks amazing. they faithfully brought over everything from the pcvr version and even improved on a lot of the mechanics. the dev team is small but passionate and actually listens to the community. 

TLDR: just buy the game already

I’ve never been more disappointed in a game.￼Combat is simple and cheap, no immersive features  or sounds even the most basic ￼￼game design features like player weapon interactions/sounds or random object interactions are missing. If you’re gonna make a $40 game these are usually expected.￼

Awesome game, good job devs! 
That being said, recently me and my buddy have been having issues on alt characters... specifically "The Disappearance" quest turn in. Worked just fine the first time thru with our mains, but any subsequent alts get stuck on the completion of the quest.  As in we did the dungeon, but the idol on the pedestal and got the gems. But when we go to turn in the quest, we can't click on it to complete it. Clicking on the quest at The Merchant just force closes the quest and doesn't allow progression.

developer_response.body:
Updates, 31th March,

Thank you again so much for taking the time to report this issue to us.

We have now completed a fix for this bug and released an update. We hope you’ll be able to update the game and continue playing without any further problems.

We also want to sincerely thank you for reporting this issue so quickly. Thanks to your bug report, we were able to respond fast and improve the experience for other players as well.

We truly appreciate your support and feedback, and we hope you continue to enjoy the game.

--- Old answer --- 

Thank you so much for purchasing and enjoying our game, and for taking the time to share your feedback with us.

We are very sorry to hear that you and your friend encountered this issue while playing on your alt characters, especially during the turn-in for “The Disappearance” quest. We understand how frustrating it must be to have your progression blocked after completing the dungeon and collecting the required quest items.

The issue you reported with the quest not completing properly at The Merchant will be shared with our development team as a high-priority bug report, and we will do our best to investigate and fix it as quickly as possible.

We sincerely apologize again for the inconvenience, and we truly appreciate your patience and support while we work on resolving this issue.

reviews.review_title: One of the Best Soulslike VR Games
reviews.review_description:
🚨 FULL REVIEW ON GAMERTAG VR YOUTUBE CHANNEL🚨

If your looking for a vr game to sink hours into, that has fun and a physically rewarding combat, tons of things to unlock and a lengthy gameplay which you can be played alone or online, Legendary tales is a game I feel many people will enjoy.
Game is great, wish DLC would come to Quest.  Please Devs reconsider and bring DLC to Quest.  Game is amazing.  Looking forward to future roadmap

Easily one of my favorite games in VR. I’ve always wanted an open style RPG adventure game and this absolutely delivers. Crafting weapons from loot you find, upgrading weapons fighting off enemies and leveling up skills. Total Banger!

If you like blade and sorcery or dungeons of eternity or even both then definitely give this a try!

Such an amazing game that I am so glad I spent the money I didn't have on lol I was very hesitant at first because of the price and me being broke but this game is worth every danged penny, and they don't even make those any more! Just love everything about this game! The magic, and weapons, and skills and story and fighting and adventuring, I could go on but you get my point lol I love games like Zelda and elden ring and this is the first game to scratch that itch since Grim lord or Asgards wrath 2.

Came back after playing for a little bit more and the legendary weapons and armor is just freaking amazing!!!! Finally tried the multiplayer and actually ran into a good team first try and had a blast! A little glitchy on multiplayer but nothing game breaking

Where is the discord?

I so expected an exceptional game at the exceptional price I paid. The tutorial and pointers along the short distance I've managed to get don't really explain anything. I can't even work out how to store any of the collected armour. It was stored one time and the next opening they vanished. The skeleton fights I've encountered are a bit pathetic. I just don't get it. I take the tutorial has to be completed before the actual game can be played because I have to start at the beginning each time I come out. And I can't find any way to go further than the jailed elf thing. I'll just give up because no game should be this stressful

developer_response.body:
Hi
I am sorry you have experienced stressful tutorials.
You just simply destroy the jail door for elf, it is explained in Quest subject.
Newest updated version has indicator to destroy the jail door since we have found some people being confused what to do.
After break the door, elf will finally teach you how to open the portal that is the last part of the tutorial.

Once you finish first tutorials, you don't need to do it ever again unless you want to do.
Also the items you have during the tutorials, it doesn't save because it is just a tutorials not the main game.

You jump into main game, there are still more things you have to learn because we have tons of features that you never experienced on other VR games.
But we teach players what is in there with sub-quests.
I know it could be stressful, but once you understand how game works, you will enjoy the game for sure.
Meanwhile, we will think about how to make tutorials better for you and others.

Warm Regards,
BJ

reviews.review_title: The RPG we all wish for VR
reviews.review_description: I played this game for so long on the PlayStation VR headset this game absolutely knocked my socks off even though I have complete the game about six times. It’s still fun to go over and do new builds with new people you got a weapon and built for any kind of player I hope you guys enjoy this. This is a game to die for.

This is a review from a Taiwan player. This is one of the most interesting VR games I've ever played. In my personal experience, I would say it's better than DOE! Thanks to the team for building this amazing game! I'm really looking forward to the support of Traditional Chinese in the Quest version.

reviews.review_title: A little too EA for my taste
reviews.review_description: I can see where the game is going and it might very well be amazing once it’s finished… in its current state it’s a little too buggy/choppy/rough  though.  The home area + inventory system is needlessly complicated. Takes forever to learn even the basics. But again, I see the potential. I’ll return in 6 months or so. I’m sure it’ll be a banger by then.

I love the combat in this. Light and heavy attacks encourage a more controlled fight. Parrying attacks feel great. Half swording lets you change the way you attack on the fly. The gestures for spell feels great. Plus I love that you can craft any of the standard gear at any time.

I absolutely love this game and I've enjoyed many play styles and met some very interesting people 😄 I was just curious when will we get the new dlc? And when will the update drop that will fix stats working on a holstered weapons?

reviews.review_title: What I always wanted from DoE and more
reviews.review_description:
Give this game a chance if you're on the fence, or even if you're not try it anyways...one thing I always wanted out of dungeons of eternity was an actual campaign I could play with my friends with progression, and this does that and more...I'll admit when I first started playing I wasn't totally impressed, but once I got the hang of the mechanics and controls I was hooked, I will now support this game any way I can and will recommend it 100%...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

reviews.review_title: The game lives up to its name
reviews.review_description:
Usually I'm skeptical when a game has a lot of hype around it, but this game proved to be one of the best games I've ever played on the Quest. 

An RPG in a massive world with physics based combat. The game has so much depth to it between the quests, loot drops, skill tree, magic, class customization, level ups, ect. This isn't your run of the mill dungeon crawler. There's more to it. You can tell real passion was put into this game. If you grew up playing g Diablo 1 or 2, then you will love this one. The price point is a little high for vr, but I think it's well worth it.

legendary tales was originally pc only, but on January 16th, 2026, it was released for quest, and i bought it the same day. i have played multiple vr games, let alone medieval/fantasy, (behemoth, ancient dungeon, blade and sorcery dungeonsof eternity) and none of them come CLOSE. i feel like people get warded off by the big price tag, and thats a shame. the graphics arent going to be the best, its a quest game, but the gameplay is one of the best ive seen and played. comparing LT to a game like blade and sorcery, its a night and day difference. blade and sorcery has better graphics, cool combat, but if you compare it to lt, it doesnt come close. the combat, the way it works when an enemy starts an animation to attack, they follow thorugh with the animation even if theyre being hit, which ive never thought of before but this game executed it and did it perfectly, the combat and bosses is so epic, and exploring feels so cool. such a great game, 5 stars, worth the money

The combat can be a lot of fun. The atmosphere is cool, with the music it definitely feels like Diablo. But the controls are amongst the worst I've ever experienced in a VR title. So much so that I find the game to be quite unplayable. Mind you - this has nothing to do with the combat, but inventory management, putting items away, the inventory system in general - it's totally busted. Makes no sense at all. Wildly inconsistent. Quite possibly the worst VR controls I've ever experienced.

I'm not sure I'm gonna be able to stick with this one.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
Thank you for playing our game.
I am sorry that you felt inconveniences.
If you don't mind, please help us to improve anything related our game by sharing your experience more.
We are opened to listen our gamer's feedback.
Join our Discord, and leave your comment.
In that way, you can help you to enjoy our game more.

Warm Regards, 
BJ

reviews.review_description:
First time playing. Excellent open world RPG. Don't expect arcade action of Dungeons of Eternity. Combat is similar to Battle Talent (maybe a tad slower), only other game I have to compare it to. There is a learning curve, so don't be discouraged. Deepest character building (skill tree) I've come across in a Quest game. Encourage you to try the free demo, Legendary Tales Prologue, before you decide. From there, you can see why the asking price.

One caveat is the separate offline/online save files. You can't bring your offline character online. You'll have two different characters. I'm one of those that try to save battery where I can, regardless of my powerbanks. So if you're constantly online whenever you're on your headset, then you won't have a problem.

reviews.review_title: It's not perfect, but it is great.
reviews.review_description:
The Standalone port is almost as good as the PC and Playstation version of the game. 

It has been made a bit easier here too, if you had trouble with the full difficulty version of the game, this one should be more accessible.

This will easily be 100+ hours of entertainment, especially if you have friends that play it too.

Plenty of different ways to make characters, I myself have 16 characters on the PC version, and 2 here so far.

Generally, narrow your scope and stack benefits to one playstyle as much as possible. Trying to do too many things will just make you bad at each thing. Still viable, but you might envy your friends characters.

The Quest version made re-specs infinite, and they re-spec everything. So you could even just have one character now. And just save completely different specs in the save slots. Like Archer, Tank, Fire mage... all on the same character.

One heads up, this version of the game so far has not sold well. Largely due to it being invisible on the store. So putting even more time, effort, and money into it to bring the expansion to it is currently looking unlikely to be feasible.

The base game as it is, is very worth it though, I mean we've all been playing it for years now. And we paid way more.

I have over 1000 hours in the PC version.

Only about 15 hours here so far, but plenty more to come.

reviews.review_title: Huge respect to the Devs for the local
reviews.review_description: "As a player from Taiwan, seeing an official Traditional Chinese update means the world to us. It makes the complex RPG mechanics so much more accessible and enjoyable. Thank you for not forgetting about us! This update has earned you a loyal fan for life. Keep up the incredible work!"

Awesome game with lots of depth, the variety of weapons, skill tree are second to none. Really enjoying playing, runs very smoothly on Quest 3 and with QGO gets a nice resolution bump. 

One suggestion would be to add an auto collection option for loot, maybe within 1m etc. Loads of loot is dropped in this game, I sometimes get a bit tired spamming my controller collecting it all 😂.

reviews.review_title: Why isn't this shown on meta's home page
reviews.review_description: This game is freaking incredible... why isn't this showing up under new releases? This game feels absolutely amazing to play... too bad Meta is making such terrible choices lately. Can't wait for Steam's new headset.

reviews.review_title: The full Quest 2 - the perfect game
reviews.review_description: I played the PC version and it's truly amazing. I didn't have high expectations for Quest 2, but it surprised me. The gameplay is identical to the PC version, and the graphics use the maximum potential of Quest 2. Even though I have both versions, I play the Quest version more; it's truly incredible.

I am an early supporter of this game, bought it on Steam and loved playing with 3 other friends.
Block, stab, parry, cast, its D&D in VR and it does not look cartoony!   Get it and play it solo or with friends.  Grind for Legendary gear or craft your own gear.

Only done the tutorial, but while it had it's impressive moments, it felt like too much hard work, figuring out how to equip the gloves and helmet for example, even when looking at the manual.

Similar with interacting with the Skeleton in cage and talking goblin.
Says 'A' to talk / interact but I have to press trigger and God knows what else to progress.

When I 1st launched the game, I was stuck in a cave with it asking me to press 'A' also.  Didn't work, in fact I couldn't even get back to the Oculus home menu to kill the game.
Had to restart the headset.

2nd time it worked and I could run the tutorial.  The battle the Skeletons to free the Goblin kinda over stayed it's welcome too, 5 waves felt too much, not difficult, just needlessly repetitive.
2 waves, with the 2nd having the larger Skeleton would have been sufficient.

Then once I'd battled the UI (harder than the enemy fight I'm given a portal spell.
After messing around back to the menu / instructions and see you need to cast and pull hands apart.

Didn't work.  I cast in both hands together, same.  I think I then pressed grip or trigger and pulled apart and that worked?

Why couldn't the Goblin have explain that to you (including controller buttons?)
I even tried the wand at my side, thinking I needed to use that, before looking in the menu etc.

This system is going to put so many  buyers off.  Dev's, I can see the promise, but you need to do a better job of easing players in.

Any VR games should give the option at the beginning to setup controller movement.

When I finally got into the tutorial, I was stuck in snap turning which I then finally could alter when allowed to get to a menu / options.

I've spend about 45 minutes in the game, and 15-20 of them felt like a frustrating fight with the system.

After finally getting in the portal, Im back in the starting cave.

Can't see a start game option.  Only tutorial or make character.

Make character and that took me to what I guess is a home village.

I saved the game and turned the headset off.  Not honestly sure if I should persist or refund at this point.

developer_response.body:
Hi, I am sorry that you have inconvenient experiences.
This precious feedback will be on our teams table and we will discuss what we could do better for you and other gamers.
I want to ask you, trust us, we will do better as you asked.
Truly sorry again.

Warm Regards,
BJ

---
Updates,
After this feedback, We have changed tutorials a bit based on your feedback.

Thank you.

reviews.review_description: The game is a lot of fun. You have to play for a while to see everything it has to offer. I would only ask the developers to also release the DLC for Quest 3.

reviews.review_title: Zero Regrets: The Ultimate Immersive RPG
reviews.review_description: It’s truly an amazing game! My friends and I all bought it with zero regrets—well worth every penny. The skill tree is incredibly immersive and in-depth. Combat feels fantastic! Awesome loot and weapons everywhere. If you’ve been hunting for a killer RPG dungeon crawler, this is it!

The game is really a different animal , unfortunately after playing for a while there are certain mechanics of the game that just don’t equal fun ,  there really isn’t anything on meta that gives you the immersive rpg dingeon crawl feeling like this game but unfortunately the price is a bit hefty 
and i hope the devs dont abandon it.

This game has everything you need in an RPG: single player campaign, option for multiplayer, different skill trees so you can play melee,  ranged and magic. Plus 50 levels, a large open world and multiple dungeons.
Am lvl 25 now, playing ice magic. Playing solo, just finished my 2nd dungeon.
Good value for money, since you can sink many hours in this game. And with Zenith and Township Tale dead there are no other RPGs left to play. I loved both of these.
It took me a while to get used to the interface, with the multiple storage options. It works, but there could be some improvements there, not loving the inventory setup and the overall menu system, it has a flat screen feel to it.
But there are a lot of nice features like the coin that creates a spirit guide that guides you to the next quest.

reviews.review_title: A Great Game for your collection
reviews.review_description: For fans of Dungeons of Eternity this game will blow you mind , what DOE lacks , crafting ,weapon creation, character editing well Legendary Tales has this and much more. Im a die hard fan of DOE but this is spectacularly fun , can be very hard and will keep you coming back for more. This game is hard solo but when you use magic for ranged shots and a sword for close melee attacks , its actually doable. 4 people multiplayer is a great group activity .This title is a must for your vr games library and the devs have done a fantastic job with it

reviews.review_title: Maybe the holy grail of VR ARPG
reviews.review_description:
Edit: The game now support TC and is much more immersive for us. Thanks a lot, BJ🙏 

I’m a player from the Taiwan VR community. Many enthusiastic players in our community highly recommended this game. I initially hesitated to buy it due to the lack of Traditional Chinese support and my tendency to get motion sickness from 'smooth locomotion' in VR. However, as soon as the developers promised Traditional Chinese support, I bought it immediately.
​I’ve played twice for about 30-60 minutes each; I still feel a bit dizzy, and there’s quite a lot of text to digest. The game design is excellent, and once the Traditional Chinese update is live, I look forward to diving back in. I hope to rediscover that deep sense of immersion I felt years ago as a student playing Ultima V in the world of Britannia.

GREAT GAME. I was aware of this game for about a year but I was totally misinterpreting the gameplay i was seeing on Steam. Now i see how the gameplay loop involves Skill and Stat point management along with Weapon Perks in creating unique builds.
And look, this is NOT like Dungeons of Eternity AT ALL. 
There's lots of exploring, dungeoning, you use weapons and gear right when you find them, adjust your build anytime, quick slot system for convenient access to spells and additional inventory, everything can kill you, light and heavy attacks actually matter.... i can go on. 
There's a lot to do here and much time can be sunk into this game. 
I've seen it compared to Diablo 2 a lot but i hadn't played that game.
Do not sleep on this one.

Played this on PCVR quite a long time ago, it's very full-featured and a great gaming experience. With the latest update (Jan 20, 2026), the tutorial is improved in many ways. The only remaining caveat is occasionally in some of the floating dialog scrolls, you'll need to click on the last part of the displayed text to get additional information, but other than that, new users shouldn't have any problems!

It took me a little bit to nail down the mechanics but after I did it I am loving it.  It's a great game that needs all the love it deserves.  If you do buy the game I suggest watching Gamer Tags tips and tricks YouTube video.  He breaks down the mechanics really well and makes it easier getting started.  Honestly I don't have a problem with the price because you get what you pay for.  Great job Devs I am looking forward to future updates.

It's basically Diablo 1 in VR. Combat is excellent. Grabbing items a little floaty but consistent. UI is functional (not great).

Developers really need to up their patch note and game changes notes.

This version has infinite stat + skill respec immediately, but previous versions (on pc and psvr2) limit respecs. Easy respecs make this version far more forgiving and much more fun to experiment or try new weapons.

I’m not a huge RPG guy, my brain does not cope with that, but this does play well for me a newbie to this type of game, just finished the tutorial and created my character and now I’m ready for action, which is the bit I love, definitely a steep learning curve for me and my limited brain power.
I really want more atmospheric music and sounds to ramp up the tension, I will update my score as I go further into the game.
Nice start though.

This game fills the void of what I was looking for in Battle Talent and Dungeons of Eternity. The skill tree is intuitive and opens up for multiple different playstyles. (The 2 handed weapon parry build being my personal favorite.) The parry system has been very satisfying and consistent. Plus, the loot system keeps me on the hunt for god rolls like in Diablo III. (A basic zombie in the dungeon dropped a Legendary 2 Handed Katana that sends out circular wind blades.) My only complaint is putting stuff in your inventory is a little cumbersome. I wish there was some mysterious, "Bag of Holding" I could pull out and throw everything into and have it auto-sort itself out. Lastly, I love the weapon grip system and every part of your weapon can be used to attack/deflect.

EDIT: Changed, "their" to, "there".

Edit 2: Aaaaah, it was pull down on Right Thumbstick and not press down on Thumbstick to loot items. . . Yeah, that was a brutal lesson to learn after 7 hours of manually unequiping items and using pouches.

I had the PCVR game and it was excellent. Now that it’s on the quest it is a dream come true. I don’t know why it took so long for it to come to the quest. It should’ve came out a long time before it did.

I love dungeons of eternity as well. It’s just that I prefer it not being based on the science fiction space dungeon crawler. I just like the traditional dungeon crawler. That’s not to say anything bad about dungeons of eternity because it is really an excellent game too.


Thank you so much for finally bringing a true dungeon crawler to the quest. It is an excellent excellent game. ￼

reviews.review_title: One of the best Meta Quest game
reviews.review_description: The Quest version is great! Congratulations to the developers. I already enjoyed the PC VR version when I played it, but now, with the new changes, the game is faster and more dynamic. I'm playing it all again with great pleasure!

reviews.review_title: Bad tutorial must be completed to play?
reviews.review_description: Already hating this. Seems I'll fumble through this horrible tutorial a few hours and have no time for the game. Imagine paying $40 for this. Seems worthy of refund so far.
This game has plenty of of content to consume. Its fun and challenging and great either solo or in co-op. worth it plus there is DLC coming soon (no i dont know a date)

I initially found out about this game through Silver Tounged Devils YouTube stream. Looked interesting enough so I purchased it. I only have about five hours in, but Im loving it so far. Graphics are good...nothing stands out as looking terrible, in fact it can look pretty good in some areas. The combat can be a little janky, but it doesn't distract from the experience. The only complaint is that the crafting and magic systems aren't well explained. I eventually figured it out though. Overall this is a must play if you like collecting stuff and adventuring.
It won't let me refund it

I am stuck on the first boss where you have to place the gem thing on the pedestal to move on. At first the gem just…disappeared, second I couldn’t pick it up, third I couldn’t pick it up. All of these times are in between restarting the game, I did it three times and each time I couldn’t pick up the gem and move on with the quest! Other than that, I really like this game and would be five stars as soon as the devs fix this!!!
Really guys, this Game is worth every penny. Buy it and enjoy it. For me its unbelievable how good the physics are.

Probably one of the best games on here close to a souls like. The only downfall is the bugs that will make legendary weapons. It has happened 3 times. Inventory was not even close to full. Hopefully they can fix this and maybe make a way where we can get back lost items through these bugs.

developer_response.body:
Hi
Thank you for playing Legendary Tales.
Did you lose Legendary Items during the game play?
It can be found in stash lost item tab that we have made for someone who lost their items.
The stash you can see in the man town/ dungeon entrance.

But if you really think its a bug, let us know on our discord with more detailed explanation, we will dig more into it.

I hope this helps you.
Warm Regards
BJ

reviews.review_description: WOW! It is amazing what a small team of developers can do. This RPG is heavy sword fighting or spell casting game using vr interactions to maximum. The focus is on character build and because of that it has huge replaybility. Fighting itself feel amazing. Parrying attacs with full physics works pretty well once you find your fight rythm. Some of the game mechanics can be hard at the beginning because some things are not fully explained in tutorial but you will learn everything as you progress the game and it is totally worth it. Its a shame that so few people know about this amazing game.
Very fun, reminds me of Diablo. Moving this from Dungeons of Eternity. Hopefully with the price point I won’t have to deal with the kids.

reviews.review_title: This is what VR was made for
reviews.review_description: If you ever dreamed of delving dungeons with friends. Throwing spells and swinging swords. This is the game for you. It is by far the best coop experience in VR I've ever done and my inner teenage nerd dances every time.
This game will definitely test your skills. There’s a lot of action and brain tease figuring it out. I definitely recommend playing this challenge.!!

reviews.review_title: MUST BUY - Best Overall RPG & Physics
reviews.review_description: I've played over 300 total hours in this game on PS2 & PC for good reason.  This is the one game you must have in your library!  MUST BUY********
to banish ghosts and goblins...on the rocky road to Dublin

I wish the party size would go to 8 players to scale to include more friends . It does scale in difficulty per person which would keep it interesting. They need more wand legendaries though and maybe a healer skill tree but overall it’s really fun and I highly recommend itit.

The main drawback is that I don't really feel a difference when attacking with a light weapon or a heavy weapon. Enemies react quite similarly regardless of the weapon used. I'd like to see more blood when their limbs are severed. Furthermore, enemies could become more aggressive under these circumstances. I'd also like to see different types of injuries: cuts from light weapons, deep gashes from heavy weapons, spear punctures, or burns. ect

I played this back when it first arrived on Steam.  I consider it the OG multiplayer VR dungeon crawler. They've made lots of improvements to this game since the original.  I count this as really the first VR dungeon crawler that truly has that RPG feel of raising stats and progression.  I also like how the combat is a little more precise and weighty, not just wave your sword as quickly as possible.  Not sure why this wasn't listed on the front meta page as a new release as I found out about the port to Quest accidently.  It's a hidden gem that needs to be unhidden.
Terrible terrible game, no refunds if you play for more than two hours, game is so slow that two hours is crap. Do not recommend!!

Great game bought it first day took me about a week to finally sit down and learn the mechanics but once you learn how to forge weapons and the crafting systems, this game is beyond spectacular. The only thing is your arms will hurt so make sure you play magic if you’re not ready for that also, I just want more more more more more weapons more dungeons more quests I just want more hopefully they keep adding more. I love this game so much. I’m afraid to beat it must play. ￼

If you are looking for hack n slash, magic blasting and bow shooting adventure this is the best around. Good combat physics but where LT truly shines is perk trees and build crafting options. If you have sunk countless hours into Oblivion or Skyrim this is the VR game for you.

reviews.review_title: Skeptical at first but I love it
reviews.review_description: Honestly when I first got the game I was iffy on it, my fiancé bought it for me because he wanted to play it and I was just so disinterested. By the time we got halfway through the first dungeon I was hooked. Been running through the game using hammers and it’s so fun. Don’t fully understand most of the RPG mechanics but still it’s incredibly fun. I’ve been running around feeling like Harley Quinn!
